item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the SCP is great, and I really enjoy your clinical tone, it makes the article seems much more realistic. I especially enjoyed the testing logs and the slow burn nature of the article, even thought it drags on a little bit at some points. Greatly enjoyed the article +1

item.title: Fresh Pine
item.content:encoded:
Currently under a bit of revision. As it was my first SCP, I've decided to put in a little more work to improve the structure.

The images used in this page were both taken and edited by me, _Utich_, and fall under CC 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article goes on for too long without providing anything to keep me invested. This is a slow-burn article that feels like a slog to sit through; I ended up skimming the experiment log halfway through. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch and thank you! I thought it'd be a fun thing to weave in.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Awesome—thanks for letting me know. This is my first SCP so I wasn't sure how adding tags normally works.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, I've cleared the tags. If you're not going to add all the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait so does this mean...
If so great job with biblical horror.

All of the hand mutilation has something to do with jesus?
